pipeline_components.architect: EdogQaLlmClient.ArchitectOnceAsync (gpt-5.4, high reasoning, strict json_schema, T1g prompt: COVERAGE BREADTH + EVIDENCE LINE PRECISION + DEFENSIVE CODE BIAS)
pipeline_components.editor: EdogQaLlmClient.EditorOnceAsync (gpt-5.4-mini, low reasoning, strict json_schema, T1g prompt: VERB SELECTION GUIDE matcher-tied + CATEGORY SELECTION GUIDE)
pipeline_components.scorer: tests/qa-eval/score_eval.py --span-expansion 15 --matcher bipartite (T1j: global bipartite matching + hunk-bounded forward expansion N=15)

notes: T1j (global bipartite matching + N=15 span expansion): macro recall lifted 0.583 -> 0.639 (+0.056 absolute, +9.6%% relative), macro precision_highest 0.661 -> 0.716 (+0.055, +8.3%%), F1_highest 0.619 -> 0.677 (+0.058). PR-975848 holds at 0.750 (T1i lift preserved), PR-976609 unlocked +1 match (0.500 -> 0.667 via s05 requiring N>=15 expansion), PR-977882 holds at 0.500 with provably-optimal assignment (no greedy pair theft). T1j replaces T1i greedy iterate-over-expected matcher with scipy.optimize.linear_sum_assignment under a cardinality-first integer-encoded objective (matches > original_overlap > expanded_overlap > deterministic tiebreaker on declared order). Cardinality dominance computed against matrix-wide totals (not per-cell) provably prevents trading two weak matches for one strong match. Forbidden cells (category/verb mismatch OR exp_overlap == 0) get weight 0 and are post-filtered. EdogQaLlmClient.cs is byte-identical to T1g (commit 0cf3903); the full T1i+T1j lift came entirely from scorer-side normalization with zero paid LLM capture. Total lift since T1g (macro_recall 0.542 -> 0.639): +17.9%% relative, +0.097 absolute. precision_validated remains 0.0 across the corpus because the scorer uses the validated-stage bucket which is structurally empty under highest-stage tagging. See score_report.json schema 1.3 for the immutable scored snapshot.
